title: Telo debug frame
description: One frame in the Telo debug wire format, delivered one-per-line over SSE (`/events`) and JSONL (`/events.jsonl`). A frame is either a kernel event (`kind: "event"`) or a line of the runtime's stdout/stderr (`kind: "log"`); consumers route on `kind` and treat a missing `kind` as an event. Event payloads are reduced to wire-safe forms first: a byte buffer (any file kind) is offloaded to a content-addressed blob store and becomes a `WireBlob` pointer (bytes fetched on demand from `/blobs/<id>`); a resolved `!ref` becomes `{ kind, name }`; any other live or unrepresentable value (controller instance, stream, client, function, bigint) becomes a `"[Marker]"` string; a reference cycle becomes `"[Circular]"`; everything else stays plain JSON. Resource lifecycle events (`<Kind>.<Name>.{Created,Initialized,Teardown}`) carry `payload.resource = { kind, name, module?, id }` where `id` is the resource's full hierarchical id (`<owner.id>/<kind>.<name>`, or `<kind>.<name>` at the top level); a resource spawned by another (a templated kind expanding into child resources) also carries `payload.owner = { kind, name, id }` and qualifies each `dependencies[].id`. A `Created` event also carries `payload.properties`: the resource's resolved config after templating (concrete values for compile-time `${{ }}`, `{kind,name}` for resolved `!ref`s, `${{ source }}` for deferred runtime expressions, and `[secret]` for scrubbed secret values). Dispatch events put the same `id` on `payload.ref` (and `owner` when applicable). A producer that omits `id` falls back to name-keyed identity, which cannot disambiguate two instances of the same templated kind.

WireBlob.title: Blob pointer
WireBlob.description: A binary value offloaded from the payload. `$blob` is a path relative to the producer origin; the consumer fetches it from `/blobs/<id>`. The object key the WireBlob sits under is preserved.

DebugEvent.title: Event frame
DebugEvent.description: A kernel event. `kind` is optional for back-compat with legacy event-only streams.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 time the producer emitted the event.

payload.description: Event payload, already reduced to wire-safe values. No fixed shape.

metadata.description: Optional producer-attached metadata (namespace, resource, kind, name, …).

DebugLog.title: Log frame
DebugLog.description: One line of the runtime's standard output/error.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 time the producer wrote the line.

stream.description: Which standard stream the line came from.

line.description: The line text (no trailing newline). ANSI escapes are preserved.
